website.verification_hint: Compare the live file with release.json on the website repository main branch. The file does not embed its own website commit.

authority_boundary: A log records what happened. A ZLAR receipt records what counted as authorized effect when action tried to become consequence.

claim_boundary.summary: Terminal-chain nested preflight refusal export while preserving the v3.4.28 nested artifact binding, the v3.4.26 readiness observed-summary contract, the v3.4.25 readiness and proof-smoke terminal-chain recognition-refusal group case-ID contract, the v3.4.24 release-forward manifest case-ID evidence, the v3.4.23 recognition-refusal group evidence, the v3.4.22 named receipt-refusal evidence, the v3.4.21 verifier-kit diagnostics boundary, and the v3.4.19 recognition-contract digest boundary.
claim_boundary.evidence_threshold: v3.4.29 release checks pass with terminal-chain nested preflight refusal export: terminal-chain reports and portable artifacts embed nested generated preflight and service-proof artifacts, and release-forward dry-run exports terminal_chain_refusal_evidence.nested_artifact_binding with forged_inner_preflight_hash_refused=true and forged_inner_service_hash_refused=true after refusing forged inner preflight and service-proof hashes while recomputing outer terminal-chain artifact integrity. Prior readiness evidence remains preserved: v3.4.25+ proof-smoke and North Star readiness preserve group_count=3, case_count=18, and exact grouped case IDs for both terminal-chain evidence and terminal-chain artifact verification. For v3.4.26+ the Enterprise Deployment Profile and Downstream Recognition Rule observed summaries mirror that same case-ID contract. v3.4.24+ release-forward verifier packets still preserve terminal_chain_refusal_evidence, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids, recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_required=true, and all_recognition_refusal_group_case_ids_preserved=true for no usable recognized receipt authority, recognized receipt scope mismatch, and route/request authority material refusal groups. v3.4.23+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve recognition_refusal_groups_sha256 and grouped before-mutation booleans. v3.4.22+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve named_receipt_refusals_sha256 and named refusal booleans for missing, invalid, stale, unknown-issuer, wrong-policy, wrong-domain, and wrong-tool receipt cases. v3.4.21+ release-forward dry-run checks still preserve zlar-verifier-kit-external-runner-diagnostics-v1.json, and v3.4.19+ readiness, proof-smoke, and release-forward dry-run checks still preserve installed-runtime-profile recognition-contract SHA-256 binding.
claim_boundary.safe_codex_wording: ZLAR can govern Codex CLI-invoked MCP tool calls when those MCP servers are routed through ZLAR.
non_claims: No hook, user, or machine configuration claim.
